expressions.english: to walk; to walk to a place; to walk away
to walk toward (a place); to come on foot
to walk fast
to walk on the street
to walk slowly
to walk without taking a break
expressions.notes: 쉬다 = to take a break; to get some rest

expressions.english: I walked for three hours.
expressions.notes:
셋 = three (native Korean number)
제 is the adjective form of 셋.

expressions.english: Shall we walk a little?
I just want to walk a little.
I walked a lot, so I'm tired.
Is it a walkable distance?
How did you walk all the way here?
I can walk from here.
expressions.notes: -는(으)면 되다 = to be just supposed to + V; to just have to + V; can just + V

expressions.english: I heard that moderate walking is good for your health.
expressions.notes:
적당히 = to be moderate
적당히 is an adverb form of 적당하다.
건강 = one's health
·대요 = I heard that + S + V; They say + S + V

expressions.english: I couldn't get a cab, so I just walked here.
expressions.notes:
택시 = cab, taxi
잡다 = to hold; to grab
택시를 잡다 = to get a cab

expressions.english: Are you really going to walk all the way there?

expressions.korean: 어제 너무 많이 걸어서 다리가 아파요.
expressions.english: I walked so much yesterday that my legs hurt.
expressions.notes:
다리 = leg
아프다 = to be sick, hurt

expressions.korean: 계속 걸어가다 보면 왼쪽에 보일 거예요.
expressions.english: If you keep walking, you will see it on your left.
expressions.notes:
계속 = repeatedly, again and again, continuously
·다 보면 = if + S + keep + V·ing
왼쪽 = left (side)
보이다 = to be seen; to be able to see

expressions.korean: 너무 많이 걸으면 피곤하니까 조금만 걸으세요.
expressions.english: Walking too much will tire you out, so walk just a little.

expressions.korean: 저는 회사에서 가까운 곳에 살아서 걸어서 출근할 수 있어요.
expressions.english: I live near my office, so I can walk to work.
expressions.notes:
회사 = company; office
가깝다 = to be near, close
가까운 is the adjective form of 가깝다.
곳 = place
살다 = to live
출근하다 = to go to work
